title: France and India Upgrade to 'Special Global Strategic Partnership' During Presidential Visit
lede: France elevated its relationship with India to 'Special Global Strategic Partnership' during President Macron's February 2026 visit, anchoring the upgrade with defence manufacturing, innovation initiatives, and critical minerals cooperation.

sections.context: The elevation of India-France ties to 'Special Global Strategic Partnership' represents a formal overarching political restatement of bilateral engagement, institutionalised through the establishment of an annual Foreign Ministers Dialogue tasked with reviewing implementation of the partnership and the Horizon 2047 Roadmap. This diplomatic upgrade arrives as both nations deepen practical cooperation across multiple strategic domains. The timing reflects sustained momentum in the bilateral relationship, with the framework now extending across defence-industrial integration, technological innovation, and critical resource security—domains central to both nations' strategic autonomy in the Indo-Pacific and globally.
sections.threads: This elevation connects to France's broader strategic positioning in the Indian Ocean region and its commitment to the Indo-Pacific framework. The constitution of a Joint Advanced Technology Development Group signals alignment with India's technological ambitions while addressing shared concerns over critical and emerging technologies. Parallel to this diplomatic architecture, France and India are institutionalising defence cooperation through the renewal of their Defence Cooperation Agreement and concrete manufacturing initiatives—the H125 Helicopter Final Assembly Line at Vemagal, Karnataka, and the joint venture between BEL and Safran for HAMMER missile production—which embed French industrial capacity within India's defence-industrial ecosystem. The launch of the India-France Year of Innovation and Innovation Network underscores technology transfer and collaborative research as central pillars, aligning with India's manufacturing and innovation policy priorities.
sections.signal: Analysts should monitor the operationalisation of the annual Foreign Ministers Dialogue as the primary mechanism for reviewing partnership implementation and the Horizon 2047 Roadmap—delays or agenda constraints would signal friction. The Joint Advanced Technology Development Group's actual research outputs and resource allocation will indicate whether this remains aspirational or becomes substantive. Defence manufacturing timelines are critical: the H125 helicopter assembly line's ramp-up and BEL-Safran HAMMER production will demonstrate India's ability to absorb and localise advanced French defence technology. Critical minerals and metals cooperation, formalised through a Joint Declaration of Intent, may yield concrete supply-chain agreements within 12-18 months as global competition for these resources intensifies. The Centre on Advanced Materials between DST and its French counterpart requires institutional commitment; progress here will reveal commitment to long-term R&D partnerships beyond diplomatic declarations.
